source: Palette sampled from Paul Klee, 'Farbtafel (qu 1)' (1930, gouache on paper); combinations composed after Klee's Bauhaus color theory in 'Beitraege zur bildnerischen Formlehre' (1921-1923), as presented in J. de Coca Leicher, 'Paul Klee. Principles on the Nature of Colour. Theory and Practice' (2018).

combinations.name: Primary Triangle
combinations.note: The pure colours at the apexes of Klee's colour triangle: red, yellow, blue at their culminating points.

combinations.name: Secondary Chord
combinations.note: The dependent colours on the triangle's sides - orange, green, violet - the Farbtafel's ultramarine standing at the violet end.

combinations.name: Blue-Orange Diameter
combinations.note: Klee's first authentic pair, the horizontal diameter of the wheel, passing through the neutral pole at the centre.

combinations.name: Red-Green Diameter
combinations.note: The second authentic pair: red against green, held in equilibrium by the pole between them.

combinations.name: Canon of Red
combinations.note: One voice of the canon of totality: the warm end of red at yellow's culmination, red culminating, the cool end falling toward blue.

combinations.name: The Cool Arc
combinations.note: Peripheral movement along the cool third of the wheel: green through blue to blue-violet, the rainbow's incomplete end.

combinations.name: The Warm Arc
combinations.note: Peripheral movement along the warm third: yellow through orange to red, each colour entering as the last culminates.

combinations.name: Illumination of Blue
combinations.note: Runge's vertical dimension, which Klee set above and below the wheel: light descending through a colour toward its dark pole.

combinations.name: Red and White Domes
combinations.note: Tunisia, 1914 - 'Colour and me, we are one. I am a painter.' After the watercolour of the same name.

combinations.name: Totalis Pentaeder
combinations.note: Klee's five-pointed totality: the three pure colours with white above and the dark pole below - the first step toward the colour sphere.

combinations.name: The Rainbow Chain
combinations.note: Five links of the seven-colour chain Klee drew from the rainbow, in their peripheral order around the wheel.

combinations.name: Blue-Orange Pendulum
combinations.note: The pendular movement Klee described on the diameter: swinging from the cool depths through the neutral pole into warmth.

combinations.name: Canon of Totality
combinations.note: The colours enter like voices in a canon: one culminates, another enters softly, a third disappears - the warm voice rising until blue answers.

combinations.name: Cosmology of the Palette
combinations.note: Klee's logical palette: the diameters blue/orange and red/green laid out as pairs, orbiting the pole at the centre.

combinations.name: Farbtafel qu 1
combinations.note: The painting's own dominant chord - the five pigments that carry most of the 1930 colour chart.
